title: Pronunciation: Numerals and Consonants
description: Learn the pronunciation rules for numbers and specific Hindi consonant combinations.

blocks.title: Pronouncing Numbers Correctly
points: एक (Ek) – The 'e' is like the 'e' in 'pet'. Do not say 'ache'.
दो (Do) – Like the English word 'dough', not 'doo'.
छह (Chhah) – The double 'h' implies a breathy ending. Some pronounce it 'chhe'.
Aspiration check: 'Chaar' starts with an unaspirated ch. 'Chhah' starts with an aspirated chh.

blocks.title: Identify the Sound Difference
blocks.instruction: Notice the subtle differences in these pairs.

pairs.diff: Notice how aspirating the 't' changes 7 into 60!

words.note: Two syllables. Nee-la (Blue). Stress is fairly even.

blocks.title: True or False — Check Your Understanding

items.statement: The number two 'दो' sounds exactly like the English word 'do'.
items.explanation: No, it sounds like the English word 'dough'.

items.statement: The Hindi word for seven (सात) ends with a soft dental 't'.
items.explanation: Yes! The tongue touches the back of the upper teeth.
